# Abhinav Atul — Personal Portfolio

> CS undergraduate at VIT Bhopal (CGPA: 8.71) and IIT Madras BS Data Science, building distributed systems, agentic AI pipelines, and real-time ML applications.

## About

Abhinav Atul is a CS undergraduate at VIT Bhopal (CGPA: 8.71) and IIT Madras BS Data Science, with experience building distributed systems, agentic AI pipelines, and real-time ML applications. Proficient in C++, Python, and Java with strong foundations in DSA, system design, and scalable backend development. Ranked in the top 3% among 43,586 contributors in GSSoC 2026, and actively contributing to open-source projects and hackathons.

## Education

- B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering — Vellore Institute of Technology, Bhopal (Aug 2024 – Expected May 2028) — CGPA: 8.71/10
- B.S. in Data Science and Application — Indian Institute of Technology, Madras (Sep 2024 – Present)
- Intermediate (Class 12) — Rankers International School, Indore (May 2022 – Jun 2024)
- Matriculation (Class 10) — Delhi Public School, Bhagalpur (Apr 2014 – May 2022)

## Technical Skills

Languages: C++ (Primary), Java, Python, SQL, JavaScript, TypeScript
Systems: Data Structures & Algorithms, Operating Systems, Distributed Systems, Concurrency, Multi-threading
Core CS: DBMS, Computer Networks, OOP
Frameworks & Tools: FastAPI, React, PostgreSQL, Supabase, OpenCV, Git, YOLOv8, Ollama
AI & ML: On-device LLM Inference (Ollama), Supervised Learning, CNN, Feature Engineering, scikit-learn

## Featured Projects

### RegisterSync AI — Regulatory Compliance Automation
Multi-agent AI platform ingesting RBI/SEBI PDFs, extracting compliance rules via locally hosted Ollama LLM, and auto-routing tasks to departments — no cloud API dependency. Features conflict-detection and verification agents with 7+ FastAPI endpoints and PyMuPDF chunking.
Technologies: Python, FastAPI, React, Ollama, PyMuPDF, PostgreSQL

### Smart Transit System
Distributed real-time transit platform with vehicle tracking, ETA prediction, and passenger analytics across concurrent data streams using 10+ RESTful APIs. Integrated YOLOv8-based crowd detection for real-time passenger flow monitoring at production-grade inference speeds.
Technologies: Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, YOLOv8

### IoT Intrusion Detection System
End-to-end ML pipeline on CICIDS2017 (2.8M+ records, 78 features) classifying traffic across 7 attack categories. Evaluated Random Forest and KNN with feature selection to handle class imbalance.
Technologies: Python, scikit-learn, Feature Engineering

### Driver Drowsiness Detection
Real-time fatigue detection pipeline using CNN and facial landmark analysis, achieving 84.9% accuracy with sub-second alert response on live video feeds.
Technologies: Python, OpenCV, CNN

### Intelligent Phishing Detector
A web app built with Streamlit that uses machine learning models like SVM and Random Forest to classify emails in real-time.
Technologies: Python, Streamlit, Scikit-learn

### UniVerse — Event Portal UI/UX
A high-fidelity prototype for a campus event portal designed from scratch in Figma during a 24-hour hackathon.
Technologies: Figma, UI/UX Design

## Experience & Activities

- LINPACK Club — Technical Team Member (Aug 2025 – Present): Contributing to internal web platform development using React and Supabase in a cross-functional team of 10+ members at VIT Bhopal.
- GirlScript Summer of Code (GSSoC) 2026 — Open Source Contributor: Ranked in the top 3% among 43,586 contributors; contributing features and bug fixes to production JavaScript/React codebases across multiple repositories.
- Smart India Hackathon 2025: Led backend development for a real-time smart transit analytics system in a 6-member team, integrating distributed data pipelines within a 36-hour build cycle.
- PyDROIDX Hackathon (Jul 2025) — Built an Intelligent Phishing Email Detection System using SVM, Logistic Regression, and Random Forest with a Streamlit web app.
- Reflux'25 Hackathon (Jun 2025) — Designed "UniVerse," a user-friendly campus event portal during a 24-hour UX design hackathon using Figma.
- SoIVIT Hackathon-2025 (Mar 2025) — University-level hackathon with 150+ teams. Advanced to Round 2 and built a community platform.
- Android Club Winter of Code (Feb 2025) — Month-long open-source fest. Secured Top 25 Contributor rank among 500+ participants.
